title: Coco Gauff Wins in Madrid While Battling a Stomach Virus
summary: Imagine trying to play tennis while feeling like you're going to throw up. That's what happened to Coco Gauff at the Madrid Open! The 22-year-old American star was battling a stomach virus. In the middle of the second set, she actually vomited into a bin. But she didn't give up! Gauff came back from a set down to beat Sorana Cirstea 4-6, 7-5, 6-1. "I don't know honestly" how she got through it, she said. "I think I got what everybody else is having here in Madrid, unfortunately." Several other players have been sick too, including Iga Swiatek, who had to quit her match. Gauff took some medicine and felt better. "Once they gave me something to help with that, then I was just nauseous and tired. But I can deal with that," she said. Next, Gauff will play Linda Noskova from the Czech Republic. Gauff is the defending champion at the French Open, which starts next month. She's using the Madrid tournament to get ready for that. Last year, she lost in the final in Madrid but then won the French Open. Go Coco!
why_it_matters: This shows that even when you feel terrible, you can still achieve your goals if you keep trying and listen to your body's needs.

keywords.explanation: Fighting hard against something difficult, like an illness.
An illness that makes your stomach feel sick and can cause vomiting.
Threw up food or liquid from your stomach through your mouth.
The person who won the tournament last year and is trying to win it again.
Feeling like you are going to throw up.

background_read: Coco Gauff (a 22-year-old tennis star from the United States) is famous for winning big tournaments and never giving up.
The Madrid Open (a major tennis tournament in Spain) takes place before the French Open, which Gauff won last year.
Iga Swiatek (a top tennis player from Poland) also got sick at the same tournament and had to stop playing.
Article_Structure: WHO: Coco Gauff, American tennis star and defending French Open champion.
WHAT: She won her match despite being sick with a stomach virus and even vomiting.
WHERE: The Madrid Open, a tournament in Madrid, Spain.
WHY: She didn't give up, took medicine, and pushed through the pain to win.

perspectives.description: She was determined to finish the match and took medicine to help her feel better, showing toughness and resilience.
It must have been tough to play against someone who was sick but still played well, and Cirstea lost despite being ahead at first.
Playing while sick can be dangerous, but with proper care and hydration, athletes sometimes manage. Gauff's recovery shows the importance of quick medical help.
